channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: NASA looking for ways to destroy asteroid that could strike Earth, kill city
item.description: NASA and other space agencies are attempting to come up with a way to destroy a massive asteroid unlikely to strike Earth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IRS plans to lay off 7,000 probationary workers beginning Thursday, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off roughly 7,000 workers in Washington and around the country beginning Thursday, a person familiar with the plans told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 things to watch at this year's CPAC
item.description: Conservatives are gathering in National Harbor, Md., this week for the annual Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in what is poised to be a celebration for the political right following President Trump’s election victory in November.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KREX 5

item.title: DOGE notches courtroom wins as Elon Musk crusades to slash federal government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Although some parts of President Donald Trump ’s agenda are getting bogged down by litigation, Elon Musk ’s Department of Government Efficiency is having better luck in the courtroom.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration halts legal aid for migrant children, leaving some to navigate courts alone
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Children so young their feet can't touch the floor when they sit in courtroom chairs have been left without representation in immigration court because of Trump administration cuts, according to organizations that help these kids navigate the legal system when they arrive in the U.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, GISELA SALOMON and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Winter-weary East Coast hit with another storm as temperatures plunge elsewhere
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Officials urged people to stay off the roads Wednesday in portions of Virginia and North Carolina where a storm dropped heavy snow and caused hundreds of accidents in places unaccustomed to significant accumulations.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Department of Homeland Security fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: 2 people are dead in a small plane collision at a southern Arizona airport
item.description: A midair collision involving two small planes in southern Arizona killed two people Wednesday morning, authorities said. Federal air-safety investigators said each plane had two people aboard when they collided at Marana Regional Airport on the outskirts of Tucson.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Measles cases are rising: What to know
item.description: A measles outbreak is happening in Texas, where there are 58 confirmed cases, and there have been confirmed cases in New Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Cynthia Erivo to host 78th Annual Tony Awards
item.description: "Wicked" actress Cynthia Erivo is set to host the 78th Annual Tony Awards. “I am so proud and excited to take on this glorious honor,” said Erivo.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Seven Chilean men are charged with burglarizing the homes of Mahomes, Burrow and other star athletes
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Seven men from Chile have been charged in Florida federal court with orchestrating burglaries at the homes of prominent professional athletes around the country, according to a criminal complaint.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Refunds delayed for taxpayers claiming Earned Income Tax Credit
item.description: The EITC helps low-to-moderate income workers and families get a tax break to reduce the taxes they owe or possibly receive a larger refund.
item.dc:creator: Brian R Svendsen, NewsNation

item.title: What's the key to surviving a plane crash? Skilled crew, strong seats and wings that detach
item.description: Experts say a combination of training and technology were critical to all 76 passengers and four crew members surviving the fiery Delta Air Lines plane crash in Toronto.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: RFK Jr. eyes childhood vaccine schedule review, says 'nothing off limits' in HHS meeting
item.description: Kennedy urged his new staff to drop any preconceived notions of who he is, but also said he wouldn’t stop asking “difficult questions” that have led to “a lot of unpopular conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin police department in 'critical need' for more officers
item.description: The Whitewater Police Department said in a Facebook post that they haven't seen an increase in police staffing for 17 years, resulting in the 'critical need' for more officers.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rosen, NewsNation

item.title: Native American activist Leonard Peltier released from prison
item.description: Leonard Peltier, a Native American rights activist, was released from a federal prison in Florida after former President Joe Biden commuted his sentence in January.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon bill would allow law enforcement to destroy hoop houses at illegal grows
item.description: Senate Bill 162 seeks to give law enforcement the authority to use heavy machinery to destroy the hoop houses — outdoor structures used to farm marijuana plants.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Arizona midair collision over regional airport between Cessna, Lancair aircraft turns fatal
item.description: An aircraft collision between Cessna and a Lancair aircraft at a regional airport in Arizona on Wednesday has killed at least two people, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tens of millions of dead people aren't getting Social Security checks, despite Trump and Musk claims
item.description: The Trump administration is falsely claiming that tens of millions of dead people over 100 years old are receiving Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE's firings and layoffs so far
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of President Donald Trump's administration as the White House and its Department of Government Efficiency fire both new and career workers, tell agency leaders to plan for “large-scale reductions
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump media company sues a Brazilian Supreme Court justice investigating Bolsonaro
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — President Donald Trump's media company sued a Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Wednesday, accusing him of violating U.S. free speech protections when he imposed a ban on a right-wing supporter of the country's former President Jair Bolsonaro.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: U.S. military tests unarmed nuclear missile in Southern California
item.description: If you were outside around 1 a.m. Wednesday on the West Coast, you may have seen or heard the U.S. military test-launching an intercontinental ballistic missile.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: USDA working to rescind terminations for fired officials working on bird flu efforts
item.description: The accidental firings come amid the Trump administration’s sweeping push to reduce the size of the government and cut the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Odds of massive asteroid striking Earth increase again: NASA
item.description: The chances of an asteroid as large as the width of an NFL field striking Earth continue to tick up, according to NASA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man drives 730 miles to burn down ex-girlfriend's new boyfriend's house, police say
item.description: "At the time the fire started, all the residents in the house were sleeping. This is obviously a nightmare for anyone," an official said.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, Fox 5

item.title: Leader of cultlike Zizians linked to 6 killings faces bail hearing in Maryland
item.description: CUMBERLAND, Md. (AP) — The leader of a cultlike group known as the Zizians that is linked to six killings in three states faces a bail hearing Tuesday along with two others who were arrested with her in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Postmaster general to step down, asks for replacement
item.description: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy, a former prominent Trump fundraiser who was appointed during the president's first term, announced Tuesday he would be stepping down.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Columbine survivor Anne Marie Hochhalter dies decades after shooting
item.description: Anne Marie Hochhalter, a survivor of the Columbine High School mass shooting, died Sunday, her former principal, Frank DeAngelis, told Nexstar's KDVR on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing hundreds of employees at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) just weeks after a deadly crash outside of Washington, D.C., put a spotlight on the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: Conservative activist Ed Martin, who echoed Donald Trump's baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol rioters, was nominated by the president on Monday to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington on a permanent basis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pope remains in hospital to treat 'complex clinical' condition: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis’ respiratory tract infection is presenting a “complex clinical picture” that will require further hospitalization, the Vatican said Monday as concerns grew about the increasingly frail health of the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: By NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: College student Jaren Barajas beats Damian Lillard in 3-point shooting contest, wins $100K
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Jaren Barajas shoots on his backyard hoop maybe once a month these days. He's a busy 18-year-old college student now, so basketball has taken a backburner to academics.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500 for Hendrick Motorsports
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla (AP) — William Byron fortuitously missed the ferocious wrecks down the stretch at the Daytona 500 that knocked out contenders racing for the checkered flag and left him with a repeat victory in sight.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500
item.description: Ninth in the No. 24 Chevrolet with one lap left, Byron was in the right place by racing near the outside wall in overtime to became the first back-to-back winner since Denny Hamlin in 2019-20.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Some in Hurricane Helene-ravaged North Carolina embrace Trump's push to abolish FEMA
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — Emily Russell remembers feeling hopeful after she managed to get an appointment with the Federal Emergency Management Agency not long after Hurricane Helene ripped though her home in Swannanoa, North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4
